Mauna Kea is taller than Everest when measured from its base.
Most of Mauna Kea rises from the seafloor, giving it a base-to-summit height over 10 kilometers.

Weak loans, conflicts, and model failures were packaged and sold as safe assets.

Cashew 'nuts' grow attached to the outside of a fleshy cashew apple.
The kidney-shaped seed hangs below the swollen fruit stalk.

Email is older than the World Wide Web.
Network email emerged in the early 1970s; the Web was proposed in 1989 and opened in the early 1990s.

Male seahorses become pregnant and give birth.
Females deposit eggs into a male brood pouch where embryos develop until birth.

A weak citation and aggressive promotion were used to minimize addiction risk.
Woolly mammoths were still alive after the Great Pyramid was built.
A small population survived on Wrangel Island until roughly 1650 BCE.
South Korea invaded North Korea in June 1950
North Korean forces crossed the 38th parallel in a planned invasion while state narratives reversed responsibility.
Saturn's average density is lower than water's.
Saturn is mostly hydrogen and helium, giving it an average density below one gram per cubic centimeter.
Apartheid was benign separate development that benefited every race
A system of disenfranchisement, forced removal, labor control, and racial domination was marketed as orderly pluralism.
The stomach lining renews itself in only a few days.
Rapid turnover protects the stomach from acid and enzymes.

Courts and asset-recovery cases documented ill-gotten wealth accumulated during dictatorship.
Lake Baikal holds about one-fifth of Earth's unfrozen surface freshwater.
Baikal's extraordinary depth and volume make it the largest freshwater lake by volume.

A pineapple is a cluster of many fruits fused together.
Each eye comes from an individual flower whose fruitlets merge.

United States and Pacific test areas

QR codes were invented to track automotive parts.
Denso Wave developed them in 1994 for fast tracking in Japanese manufacturing.

Clownfish can change sex, with the dominant male becoming female.
Clownfish live in hierarchies; when the female dies, the breeding male transitions to female.

Investigations found an al-Qaeda plot; 'inside job' claims rely on misrepresented engineering and intelligence evidence.
Nintendo was founded in the year the Eiffel Tower opened.
Nintendo started making playing cards in 1889, the same year the tower was completed and inaugurated.
LIBOR submissions reflected banks' real borrowing costs
Banks submitted false rates to improve trading positions or appear healthier.
The adult skeleton is continuously dismantled and rebuilt.
Osteoclasts and osteoblasts remodel bone throughout life; much skeletal tissue is renewed over roughly a decade.
The 2020 U.S. presidential election was stolen through massive coordinated fraud
Courts, audits, officials, and the Justice Department found no fraud capable of changing the result.
Antarctica's ice sheet contains roughly 90 percent of Earth's ice and about 70 percent of its freshwater.
The enormous Antarctic ice sheet stores the dominant share of frozen freshwater.
Volkswagen's 'clean diesel' cars met emissions rules in normal operation
Defeat-device software detected tests while vehicles emitted far more nitrogen oxides on the road.
A fig's flowers bloom inside the structure people eat.
The fig is an enclosed inflorescence lined with tiny internal flowers.

Bluetooth is named after a Viking-age Danish king.
Engineers used Harald Bluetooth, who united Danish regions, as a metaphor for uniting devices.

Male platypuses have venomous ankle spurs.
Adult males can deliver painful venom through spurs on the hind legs.

A working fax-machine concept was patented before the telephone.
Alexander Bain patented an electric printing telegraph in 1843; Bell's telephone patent came in 1876.

Mass killing, rape, village destruction, and displacement were denied or sharply minimized.
The Moon is moving away from Earth by about 3.8 centimeters per year.
Laser ranging of lunar reflectors measures the gradual tidal recession.

The DNA in one human cell would be about two meters long if stretched out.
DNA is wrapped around proteins and folded into chromosomes inside the nucleus.

Hundreds of liquid lakes exist beneath Antarctica's ice.
Pressure, geothermal heat, and insulation keep subglacial lakes liquid.

Commercial vanilla beans are cured seed pods of orchids in the genus Vanilla.

Wi-Fi does not officially stand for 'Wireless Fidelity.'
The name was created as a brand; 'Wireless Fidelity' was a later marketing phrase.
